originalTitle: Nigeria warns its citizens in South Africa to be cautious after march turns violent
title: News from Far Away: Being Kind and Staying Safe!
content:
Hello, Amazing Kids!

Hello, super friends! It’s your kid-friendly news reporter here, bringing you important stories from our wonderful continent, Africa! Today, we’re going to talk about some news from a country called South Africa. It’s a bit serious, but don't worry, we'll explain it simply, and the most important thing is always to remember to be safe and kind! Sometimes, grown-ups can have disagreements, just like sometimes you and your friends might not agree on which game to play. But it’s super important to always be kind and listen to each other. That’s what we want to remember today.

What Happened in East London?

Imagine you have a group of friends, and you need someone to help make decisions or plan fun activities. That person would be your leader, right? Well, in a city in South Africa called East London, there are many people from Nigeria. These people are called the Igbo people. They are a big, friendly group, and they like to have leaders to help them. Recently, a man named Solomon Ogbonna Eziko was going to become a special leader for the Igbo people in East London. It's like having a new class captain or a new leader for your playgroup. Leaders are very important because they help everyone in their group. They listen to problems and try to make things fair. But here’s the tricky part: not everyone was happy about this new leader. Sometimes, people have different ideas about who should be a leader. And when people have different ideas and get very upset, it can cause some trouble.

When Friends Disagree

When people were unhappy about the new leader, some of them decided to walk together and show how they felt. This is called a 'march.' It’s a way for many people to show they want something different to happen. Usually, marches are peaceful, which means everyone walks calmly and just shows their feelings with signs or songs. But sadly, in East London, this march didn't stay peaceful. Some people started getting very, very upset. Things became a bit messy and even a little bit scary. This is what we call 'violence,' and it means people were not being kind or safe to each other. They were fighting or breaking things, which is never a good way to solve problems. Did you know? South Africa is a country that is very far south in Africa! It’s famous for its beautiful animals like lions, elephants, and giraffes! It also has 11 official languages!

Nigeria Wants Everyone to Be Safe

Now, because some people got upset and things got a bit unsafe, the country of Nigeria wanted to make sure its people living in South Africa were okay. Imagine your parents telling you to be extra careful when you're playing outside, or to stay close to them in a crowded place. That’s exactly what Nigeria did! Nigeria told its people, its 'citizens,' who live in South Africa to 'be cautious.' Being cautious means being extra careful, looking around you, and staying away from places where there might be big, noisy crowds or people who are upset. It's like listening to your parents when they tell you to stay away from a busy road – they just want you to be safe! This warning is very important. When your country tells you to be careful, it means they care about you and want to keep you out of trouble. They want everyone to come home safely at the end of the day.

Why Leaders Are So Important

Leaders are like the captains of a team or the head teacher at your school. They have a big job to do. They need to listen to everyone, try to make fair choices, and help solve problems so that everyone can live happily and safely together. When people disagree about a leader, it can be really hard. But the best way to solve problems is always to talk, listen, and try to understand each other, not to get angry or fight. We learn this in school every day, don't we? Sharing and taking turns are good ideas for grown-ups too! Did you know? Africa is the second-largest continent in the world! It has 54 different countries, each with its own amazing cultures and traditions!

Learning from This News

So, what can we learn from this news, my young friends? We learn that it’s important to: 1. **Be careful:** Always listen to grown-ups when they tell you to be safe. If you see big crowds or people who are very upset, it’s best to stay away and tell an adult. 2. **Be kind:** Even when people disagree, it’s important to try and be kind. Talking is always better than fighting. 3. **Understand others:** Sometimes, people have different ideas, and that's okay. We can try to understand why they feel the way they do. 4. **Leaders help:** Leaders have a big job to make sure everyone in their group is happy and safe. Let's all hope that the people in South Africa can solve their disagreements peacefully and that everyone stays safe. Our big African family is full of wonderful people, and we always wish for peace and happiness for everyone. Keep exploring, keep learning, and most importantly, keep being kind! See you next time for more amazing news from Africa!
summary: Nigeria has told its people living in South Africa to be careful after some grown-ups got upset and a peaceful walk turned messy when a new leader was chosen in a city called East London.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us about being careful when people disagree and how important it is for everyone to stay safe and try to be kind, even when things are difficult.

originalTitle: Tuanzebe sends DR Congo back to World Cup after 52 years
title: Tuanzebe sends DR Congo back to World Cup after 52 years
content: DR Congo qualify for their first World Cup since 1974 as Axel Tuanzebe's strike gives the Leopards a 1-0 win over Jamaica in their play-off.

originalTitle: Sexual violence part of 'everyday life' in parts of Sudan, charity says
title: Keeping Our Friends Safe in Sudan
content:
Hello, brave reporters! Today, we have an important story about a special country in Africa called Sudan. It's a land with beautiful deserts, big rivers, and lots of wonderful people. But sometimes, even in lovely places, people can face big challenges. Let's learn about how helpers are trying to make things better for everyone there.

Everyday Life Can Be Tricky

Imagine your day. You wake up, maybe have some yummy breakfast, and get ready for school. You might help your grown-ups fetch water, or go to the market to buy food. These are things we do every single day, right? But for some kids and grown-ups in parts of Sudan, doing these simple, everyday things can be very scary. Sometimes, there are big problems and fighting in parts of Sudan. When there is fighting, bad people can make others feel unsafe. People who just want to live their lives, like mothers going to get water for their families or grandmas going to the market, sometimes get hurt or scared. They tell stories about feeling very worried when they try to do these normal things. This makes many people sad and afraid to even leave their homes. Did you know? Sudan is a very big country, the third largest in Africa! It has a long history and many different kinds of people living there.

Helpers Are There to Listen and Care

There are many kind people in the world who want to help when others are hurting or scared. In Sudan, there are special groups, like a charity (which is a group of people who help others for free), working very hard. These helpers listen to people who have felt scared or hurt. They want everyone to know that what happened to them was not okay, and it was not their fault. These helpers try to make safe places for people, especially for women and children. A safe place is somewhere you can feel calm, where you know no one will hurt you, and where you can share your feelings. They offer comfort and try to make people feel strong again. It's like having a kind teacher or a grown-up who always makes you feel safe and loved. Did you know? Charities around the world do amazing work, helping people with food, water, schools, and safety. They are like real-life superheroes, but without capes!

Working for a Brighter Future

The people working for these charities want to remind everyone that every single person deserves to be safe and happy. No one should ever feel scared when they are just trying to live their life, get food, or fetch water. They teach everyone that being kind and respectful to each other is very important. They also teach that if you ever feel unsafe or if someone treats you badly, it’s super important to tell a grown-up you trust. This could be your mom, dad, grandma, grandpa, a teacher, or another trusted grown-up. Telling someone helps you and can help others too. Did you know? The River Nile, one of the longest rivers in the world, flows through Sudan! Many people in Sudan depend on it for water and farming. The helpers in Sudan are working very hard so that one day, every child can go to school, every grown-up can go to the market, and everyone can fetch water without feeling any fear. They believe that with kindness, listening, and working together, Sudan can become a place where everyone feels safe and can live their lives happily. We can all send kind thoughts to the people of Sudan and hope for peace and safety for everyone there. Remember, being kind and helping each other makes the world a better place for all! Did you know? Children like you can help too! By being kind to your friends, sharing, and being a good listener, you help make the world a kinder, safer place for everyone. Every little act of kindness counts!
summary: Helpers in Sudan are working hard to keep people safe and help them feel happy again, especially when bad things make them scared to do everyday tasks like getting water or going to the market.
whyMatters: It's important because everyone, everywhere, deserves to feel safe and happy, and when people are scared or hurt, we need to help them so they can live their lives without fear.

originalTitle: Residents inspect drone damage near Erbil airport
title: Residents inspect drone damage near Erbil airport
content: Residents living near Erbil's international airport in northern Iraq, say falling drone debris has damaged properties.

title: A Brave Storyteller is Missing!
content:
Hello, My Wonderful Friends!

Hello, my wonderful friends! It's your friendly news reporter from Africa here, ready to share an important story with you. Sometimes, news can be a little bit sad, but it's always good to know what's happening in the world, even far away from our homes. Knowing things helps us understand and care for each other.

Meet Shelly: A Special Storyteller

Today, we are talking about a kind person named Shelly. Shelly is a special kind of helper. She is what we call a journalist. Do you know what a journalist does? They are like storytellers! They travel to different places and find out what is happening. Then, they write stories or make videos to tell everyone else. Shelly helps people know about the world. She finds interesting things and shares them so we can all learn. Imagine someone who loves to ask questions, like 'Why is the sky blue?' or 'How do birds fly?' Journalists ask questions about important things happening in cities and countries.

Did You Know?

**Did you know?** There are many brave journalists right here in Africa! They tell us about new schools, amazing animals, and fun festivals in our own countries. They help us learn about our neighbors and our beautiful continent. Journalists help us feel connected to the big, wide world!

What Happened to Shelly?

But something has happened to Shelly. She was in a big city called Baghdad, very far from Africa. And while she was doing her work, she was taken away by some people. This happened in the daytime, when the sun was shining brightly, for everyone to see. The word 'kidnapped' might sound a little bit scary, but let's understand what it means in a simple way. It means someone was taken away from where they were, without their permission or the permission of their family. It's like someone took your toy without asking, but it's much more serious because it's a person. This is not a kind thing to do. Everyone should be safe and free to go where they need to go, especially when they are doing good work like Shelly. This happened in 'broad daylight.' That just means it happened when the sun was up, not at night. It's important to know that many people are looking for Shelly now.

Where is Baghdad?

Now, where is this city Baghdad? It's very, very far away from us in Africa. Imagine looking at a big map of the world. Africa is on one side, and Baghdad is in a country called Iraq, on another side. It's a big city with many people and many buildings, just like some of our big cities in Africa. Shelly was there to find stories, just like our local journalists find stories in our towns. She wanted to share news from that part of the world with other people. Knowing about other places helps us understand everyone better.

Did You Know?

**Did you know?** Iraq is a country with a very long history, just like some countries in Africa! It has very old cities and interesting traditions. Learning about different places helps us see how big and wonderful our world is.

Why is Shelly Important?

Shelly, like all journalists, helps us to know things. When we know things, we can understand the world. We can learn about new foods, different animals, and how people live in other places. This makes our world bigger and more interesting! When a storyteller like Shelly goes missing, it makes many people sad and worried. Her family is worried, and her friends are worried. Everyone hopes she will come back safely soon. It's like if your favorite teacher or a kind helper in your community suddenly wasn't there. You would wonder where they are and hope they are okay, right?

What is a 'Freelance' Journalist?

The news said Shelly is a 'freelance' journalist. That's a big word, but it just means she works for herself! She doesn't work for one big news company all the time. She chooses the stories she wants to tell and then offers them to different newspapers or websites. It's like being your own boss and choosing your own adventures to write about. This makes her very independent and brave, going to places to find stories that she thinks are important to share with the whole world.

Staying Safe and Hope

It's a good reminder for all of us that safety is very important. Always stay with your grown-ups, and if you ever feel unsafe, tell a trusted adult immediately. We all look out for each other, don't we? The news about Shelly reminds us that even brave people need our thoughts and hopes for their safety. Many people are working hard to find Shelly and bring her home. We hope that Shelly will be found soon and be safe with her family again. Let's send our kind thoughts to Shelly and her family from here in Africa. Remember, friends, sharing stories and learning about the world makes us all stronger. And everyone deserves to be safe while doing good work. That's all for today's news. Keep shining bright!
summary: A brave storyteller named Shelly went missing in a far-off city called Baghdad, and many people hope she will be found safely soon.
whyMatters: It is important because everyone, especially people who help us learn new things, should be safe and free to do their good work without worry.

originalTitle: Judge halts Trump ballroom project; Trump says work can go on
title: Judge halts Trump ballroom project; Trump says work can go on
content: Donald Trump seemed to suggest work on the $400 million White House ballroom could continue despite a judge’s order.

title: Iran using children in security roles in war, reports and witnesses say

originalTitle: Israel says it will keep control over part of southern Lebanon after war with Hezbollah ends
title: Thinking About Peace and Safety!
content:
Welcome, Little Superstars!

Hello, hello, my amazing friends! It's your favorite kid-friendly news reporter here, ready to share some interesting news with you from our big, beautiful continent of Africa and beyond! Today, we're going to talk about something very important: how grown-ups try to keep everyone safe and happy. Imagine you are playing with your best friend. Sometimes, you might have a little disagreement, right? But then you talk about it and find a way to play nicely again. Countries are a bit like that too, but on a much bigger scale! Our news today is about two places that are neighbors, just like you might have a neighbor next door. These places are called Israel and Lebanon. They are in a part of the world that is far away from many of us in Africa, but still, we want to understand what's happening.

What's Happening with Our Neighbors?

So, there are some grown-ups in the country called Israel. They are talking about what they want to do to help keep people safe. They want to make sure that a special part of land, which is in their neighbor country, Lebanon, stays peaceful and calm. This part of Lebanon is very close to Israel's own land. It's like when you have a fence between your garden and your neighbor's garden. You both want to make sure that everything on both sides of the fence is safe and sound. These grown-ups in Israel are thinking about how to make sure everyone feels very secure after a time when there was some trouble. They are making plans for a future where people can live without worries, especially near the line that separates the two countries.

Why Do Grown-Ups Talk About 'Safety Zones'?

Sometimes, grown-ups use words like "control" or "security." But for us, let's think about it like this: it means they want to make sure an area is a very safe place. It's like when your mom or dad helps you cross the road. They take your hand and make sure no cars are coming. They are helping you stay safe, right? The grown-ups in Israel want to make sure that the area close to their border, in southern Lebanon, is a place where no one has to be scared. They want it to be a quiet zone, a happy zone, where children can play and families can live without any problems. This is their big wish! Did you know? A 'border' is just an imaginary line on a map that shows where one country ends and another begins! It's not a real fence you can always see.

A Special Place: Southern Lebanon

Let's imagine a beautiful green area with small villages and perhaps some friendly animals. This is what southern Lebanon can be like. It's a lovely place where people live. This area is very, very close to the country of Israel. Because they are so close, the grown-ups want to make extra sure that nothing bad happens right there. They are thinking about what needs to happen so that people on both sides of that border can wake up every day feeling happy and safe. It's a big job for grown-ups to make sure everyone is secure, isn't it? They have many meetings and think very hard about these things. Did you know? Lebanon is famous for its beautiful cedar trees! They are even on their flag!

Plans for Making Places Safer

Now, here's a part that sounds a little different, but it's still about safety. The grown-ups are also talking about some buildings in those villages close to the border. They are saying they want to make some changes to these buildings. It's not because they don't like the houses, but because they believe that changing them will help make the area super-duper safe for everyone. Think of it like this: sometimes, when you have an old toy that's broken, your parents might fix it or get a new one to keep you safe from sharp edges. Or, maybe they want to make an open field instead of an old building, so it's easier to see everything and make sure no one is hiding to cause trouble. They want to create a kind of "peace buffer" – a special zone where everyone knows it's a place for quiet and no problems. This means they might make changes to ensure no buildings can be used to harm people. This is a way to try and stop any future disagreements or problems from starting near the border. It's all about making sure people can live in peace. Did you know? A 'buffer zone' is like a peaceful space between two busy places, making sure they don't bump into each other!

Why is Safety So Important?

Safety is like a warm blanket on a cold night. It makes you feel protected and cared for. Every child and every family deserves to feel safe in their homes and their countries. When people feel safe, they can go to school, play with friends, help their families, and dream big dreams. When grown-ups talk about making special plans for borders, it's always because they want to protect their people and make sure everyone can live without fear. It's a very big responsibility, and they are trying their best to find the right ways to do it. Imagine if you could build a giant invisible shield around your home that kept everyone safe. That's what these plans are trying to do, in a grown-up way! Did you know? In many African countries, children learn about peace and friendship from a very young age, often through stories and songs!

Our Hope for the Future

Even when grown-ups talk about making plans for safety, our biggest hope is always for peace. We hope that one day, all neighbors, everywhere in the world, can live together like the best of friends. We hope that children in Israel and children in Lebanon can grow up playing, laughing, and learning, side-by-side, without any worries. It's important to remember that most people in the world, no matter where they live, just want to be happy and live peacefully with their families. These plans are steps that grown-ups believe will lead to a more peaceful tomorrow for everyone in that part of the world. We in Africa send our warmest wishes for peace and understanding to all people, everywhere!

More Fun Facts About Our World!

Did you know? The continent of Africa has 54 countries! That's a lot of neighbors living together! Did you know? Many countries have special places called "national parks" where animals and nature are protected and kept safe. Just like grown-ups try to keep people safe! Did you know? The Mediterranean Sea is a big, beautiful sea that touches the shores of both Israel and Lebanon! It's like a shared swimming pool for many countries!

Let's All Be Friends!

So, remember, when you hear about big news, it often comes down to grown-ups trying to make the world a safer, happier place for everyone, especially for children like you! Let's all keep hoping for a world where everyone can live in peace, share smiles, and be kind neighbors to each other. That's all for today from your news reporter! Stay curious, stay kind, and shine bright!
summary: Grown-ups in Israel are making plans to help keep a special area near the border with Lebanon very safe and peaceful for everyone.
whyMatters: It's important because everyone wants to live in a safe place where there is peace and no worries. These plans are about making sure children and families can be happy and secure in their homes.

title: US journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad
content: The US state department says a suspect in her abduction has ties to an Iran-backed militia group, Kataib Hezbollah.

content: England's World Cup hopes rest on captain Harry Kane after a grim taste of life without him in the defeat against Japan at Wembley, says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

title: 'Scotland fans can fret - but they need to keep perspective too'

originalTitle: Angry Wales boss Bellamy shows his new old self
title: Angry Wales boss Bellamy shows his new old self
content: Craig Bellamy showed his fiery temper for the first time as Wales boss in their Northern Ireland draw - a rage that was evident during his playing days.
